title: Modular Arithmetic and Public-Key Reasoning · Language Lens: Crypto Go Gcd Inverse
summary: An implementation team manipulates big integers without normalizing signs, confuses modular inverse with division, and mistakes a worked toy example for secure key generation. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gcd inverse increment in bounded modular arithmetic reasoning notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Use Euclid and extended Euclid to determine coprimality and modular inverses and identify non-invertible cases.
Compute and explain modular equivalence, reduction, addition, multiplication, exponentiation, and negative representatives.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
